Google 


This  is  a  digital  copy  of  a  book  that  was  preserved  for  generations  on  library  shelves  before  it  was  carefully  scanned  by  Google  as  part  of  a  project 

to  make  the  world's  books  discoverable  online. 

It  has  survived  long  enough  for  the  copyright  to  expire  and  the  book  to  enter  the  public  domain.  A  public  domain  book  is  one  that  was  never  subject 

to  copyright  or  whose  legal  copyright  term  has  expired.  Whether  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  may  vary  country  to  country.  Public  domain  books 

are  our  gateways  to  the  past,  representing  a  wealth  of  history,  culture  and  knowledge  that's  often  difficult  to  discover. 

Marks,  notations  and  other  maiginalia  present  in  the  original  volume  will  appear  in  this  file  -  a  reminder  of  this  book's  long  journey  from  the 

publisher  to  a  library  and  finally  to  you. 

Usage  guidelines 

Google  is  proud  to  partner  with  libraries  to  digitize  public  domain  materials  and  make  them  widely  accessible.  Public  domain  books  belong  to  the 
public  and  we  are  merely  their  custodians.  Nevertheless,  this  work  is  expensive,  so  in  order  to  keep  providing  tliis  resource,  we  liave  taken  steps  to 
prevent  abuse  by  commercial  parties,  including  placing  technical  restrictions  on  automated  querying. 
We  also  ask  that  you: 

+  Make  non-commercial  use  of  the  files  We  designed  Google  Book  Search  for  use  by  individuals,  and  we  request  that  you  use  these  files  for 
personal,  non-commercial  purposes. 

+  Refrain  fivm  automated  querying  Do  not  send  automated  queries  of  any  sort  to  Google's  system:  If  you  are  conducting  research  on  machine 
translation,  optical  character  recognition  or  other  areas  where  access  to  a  large  amount  of  text  is  helpful,  please  contact  us.  We  encourage  the 
use  of  public  domain  materials  for  these  purposes  and  may  be  able  to  help. 

+  Maintain  attributionTht  GoogXt  "watermark"  you  see  on  each  file  is  essential  for  in  forming  people  about  this  project  and  helping  them  find 
additional  materials  through  Google  Book  Search.  Please  do  not  remove  it. 

+  Keep  it  legal  Whatever  your  use,  remember  that  you  are  responsible  for  ensuring  that  what  you  are  doing  is  legal.  Do  not  assume  that  just 
because  we  believe  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  the  United  States,  that  the  work  is  also  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  other 
countries.  Whether  a  book  is  still  in  copyright  varies  from  country  to  country,  and  we  can't  offer  guidance  on  whether  any  specific  use  of 
any  specific  book  is  allowed.  Please  do  not  assume  that  a  book's  appearance  in  Google  Book  Search  means  it  can  be  used  in  any  manner 
anywhere  in  the  world.  Copyright  infringement  liabili^  can  be  quite  severe. 

About  Google  Book  Search 

Google's  mission  is  to  organize  the  world's  information  and  to  make  it  universally  accessible  and  useful.   Google  Book  Search  helps  readers

Of  thin  Catalogue  Part  2  (Periodicals).  Part  3  (MiiRio),  Part  4  (Engravings),  will  be  iHsued  monthly 
hereafter,  instead  of  weekly.    Part  1  (Books)  will  be  issued  every  week  as  heretofore. 

The  weekly  issue  of  (he  numbers  of  Vnn»  2,  3,  and  4  necessitated  search  through  several  short  alpha- 
bets in  order  to  locate  any  given  title.  To  avoid  this  inconvenience  it  has  been  suggested  that 
they  he  published  monthly,  so  that  a  search  through  a  year  of  the  Catalogue  will  require  the 
examination  of  only  12  indexes  instead  of  52  as  formerly. 

The  last  weekly  i.ssue  of  Part  3  (l^ngnivinKs.  ChromoH.  Photographs,  Fine  Arts)  was  on  Octol)er  18, 
1906.    The  tir.^t  monthly  issue  is  under  date  of  November  l'\  1906. 

To  mve  space  ihe  copyrighi  noti<;e  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first 
title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read,  "Copyright  bv  George  F.  Adams,  FortresH  Mtmroe,  Va.  Clasa

Number  of  entries  of  Photographs  completed  by  the  deposit  of  2  copies  since 
Jan.  1,  1907 4,887 


CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 


Under  this  heading:  is  given  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with  applications 
for  copyright  of  palntins:!!,  drawing!*,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs  intended  to  be  perfected  as 
works  of  xhe  One  arts.    Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors;  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first 
title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read,  "Copyright  by  Marv  B.  Adelsperger,  Chicago,  111.  Class  1, 
XXc  no.  20968,  Feb.  20, 1907;  1  photograph  received  Feb.  20, 1907." 


Adelsperger  (Mary  B.),  Chi- 
cago, III. 
Alice.    Ba»-relief,  circular,  about  4  inches 
in  diameter.     Full  face  view  of  young 
child,  right  upper  arm  and  shoulder

Tbe  Cataiogne  of  Copyright  Entries  appeais  indt^vreek.  As  tb^  issue  of  Part  4  would  necessitate  tli0 
printing  of  a  namoer  of  small  atpbabets  for  the  different  subject-matters  contained  in  this  Part— 
namelT,  Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos.  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine  Art*— 
it  has  Men  suggested  that  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets 
this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be  made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  etxh  month  instead  of 
each  week  for  Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fins 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  £2  indexes  in  the  case  of  each  subject-matter, 
it  will  now  be  necessary  to  ftTamine  only  12  indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated;  the  statement  as  to  receipt  of 
eoi^  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  same  day  as  the  day  of  entry.  The 
notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read, "  Copyright  by  American-Joumai-examiner, 
Hew  York,  N.  Y.    Class  F,  XXc,  no.  £0067,  Feb.  26, 1907;  2  copies  received  Feb.  26, 1907."

far  eopyri^t  of  paixitings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs  intended  to  be  perfected  as

ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS,  AMIpr  PmNTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPH^-;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


r, 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS.  CUTS.  AWD  PRINTS 

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Entries  appeal  each  week.  As  the  issue  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-matters 
contained  in  this  Part — ^namely,  Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs.  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — it  has  been  suggested  that  to  avoid  the  incon- 
venience of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  o2  Indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  it  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  Indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  aa  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read,  *'  Copyright  by  Alpha 
Delta  Phi,  Macon,  Ga.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  52628,  Apr.  10,  1907;  2  copies  received 
Apr.  10.  1907."

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  sabalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  The  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  2  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day 
of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full,  would  read :  "  Copyright  by 
James  E.  Abbe,  Newport  News,  Va.  Class  H,  XXc,  no.  93035,  Apr.  26,  1907 ;  2  copies 
received  Apr.  26,  1907."

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 

Under  this  headlnis;  is  given  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  palntlngSt  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
intended  to  be  perrected  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  snoalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  Is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full,  would  read :  '*  Copy- 
rlglit  by  Joel  Nott  Allen,  New  York.  N.  Y.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  21361 ;  Apr.  1,  1907 ;  1 
photograph  received  Apr.  1,  1907."

ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS,  AND  PRINTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPHS;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

The  Catalosae  of  Copyright  Entries  appears  each  week.  As  the  Issae  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-matters 
contained  In  this  Part — ^namely,  Bngraylngs,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — It  has  been  suggested  that  to  ayold  tne  Incon- 
Tenlence  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  Instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithograpns,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  62  indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  it  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  saye  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry- 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read,  "  Copyright  by  J.  P. 
Acomb,  Cincinnati,  Ohio.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  64160,  May  11,  1907 ;  2  copies  received 
Apr.  29,  1007."

nati, Ohio.

To  ULTe  space,  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreylated ;  the  statement  as  to

entry.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full,  would  read :  Copyright 
by  Conrier  company  of  Buffalo,  Buffalo,  N.  x.  Class  O,  XXc,  no.  15778,  May  11,  1907 ;

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 

Under  tbls  heading  Is  given  a  Hat  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
Intended  to  be  perfected  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  saoalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry,  l^e  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  **  Copy- 
r^t  by  American  lithographic  company,  New  York,  N.  T.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  21961, 
May  25,  1907 ;  1  photograph  received  May  25,  1907.''

ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS,  AND  PRINTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPHS;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS.  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Entries  appears  each  week.  As  the  Issue  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  of  smaU  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-matters 
contained  In  this  Part — ^namely,  Bngravlngs,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — It  nas  been  suggested  tbat  to  ayold  the  Incon- 
Yenlence  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  Instead  of  each  week  for 
Bngrayings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  52  indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  It  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  Indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  If  printed  in  full,  would  read,  "  Copyright  by  W.  C. 
Allen,  Brooklyn,  N.  Y.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  54710,  May  31,  1907  ;  2  copies  received  May 
22.  1907." 


Allen   (W.  C),  Brooklyn,

CLASS  G.  CHROMOS  AND  LITHOGRAPHS 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space,  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreyiated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.  Thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  wonld  read :  Copyright 
by  American  motor  car  manufacturers'  association.  New  York,  X.  Y.  Class  G,  XXc,  no. 
14991,  Jan.  2,  1907  ;  2  copies  received  Feb.  26,  1907.

CLASS  I,  FINE  ARTS 


Under  this  heading  is  given  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
intended  to  be  perfected  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreylated;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 


day  of  entry.    The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read:  "Co 


right  by  Allcock  manufacturing  company,  New  York,  N.  Y.     Class  I, 
Jane  6,  1907 ;  1  photograph  received  June  6,  1907."

ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS,  AND  PRINTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPHS;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Bntrles  atHpeanr  each  week.  As  the  Issue  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-matters 
contained  in  this  Part — ^namely,  Bngravlngs,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs.  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — it  nas  been  suggested  that  to  ayold  the  Inoon- 
Tenlence  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  Instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts.  Prints,  Chromos,  Llthograpns,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  tnerefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  52  Indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  it  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreyiated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  "  Copyright  by  Ina  M.  C. 
Adams,  Baltimore,  Md.  Class  F,  aXc,  no.  56218,  July  13,  1907 ;  2  copies  received  July 
13,  1907."

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  sare  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated.  The  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  2  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day 
of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copyright  by 
Arthur  Russell  Allen.  Trinidad,  Colo.  Class  H,  XXc,  no.  94477,  May  24,  1907 ;  2  copies 
received  June  24,  1907."

CLASS  I,  FINE  ARTS 

Under  thlB  heading  Is  sriven  a  Hat  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
appllcatlonB  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
Intended  to  be  perfected  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  snbalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreTlated ;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  Is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title.  If  printed  In  full,  would  read :  "  Copy- 
right by  American  cigar  company,  New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  I,  X^c,  no.  22414,  July  17, 
1907 ;  1  photograph  received  July  17,  1907."

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Bntrlea  apoean  each  week.  As  the  lisue  of  Part  4  would 
necestltaM  the  printing  of  a  number  or  small  alphabets  for  the  different  sabject-mattars 
contained  in  this  Part — ^namely.  Bngrayinn.  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos.  Lithographs.  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — it  has  been  suggested  that  to  aToid  the  inoon- 
Tenience  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  instead  of  each  week  for 
BngraTlngs,  Cuts.  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  62  Indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  it  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  Indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  saye  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreriated;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  receiyed  on  the  day  of  entir. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full  would  read,  '•  Copyright  by  WUmer  B. 
Adams,  Fort  Warren,  Mass.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  56806,  Aug.  5,  1007 ;  2  copies  received 
July  29,  1907."

received  Aug.  7,  1907."

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 


Under  this  headlni;  is  giyeo  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
intended  to  he  perfectea  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.     Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 

.   prletors  with  sabalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title.  If  printed  in  full,  would  read :  '*  Copy- 
right by  Raymond  Francis  Almlrall,  New  York.  N.  Y.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  22732,  Aug. 
22.  1007 ;  1  photograph  received  July  31,  1907."

CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS.  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

Tlie  Catalosoe  of  Copyrtsbt  Entries  appears  each  week.  As  the  Issue  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  prlutlng  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-matters 
contained  In  this  Tart — namely,  EnKravln^s.  Cuts,  Trlnts,  Chromos,  Lltbo»;raphs,  I'ho- 
tOKi'apha,  and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — It  has  been  suKKested  that  to  avoid  the  Incon- 
venience of  searching  such  a  numl)er  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalo)?ue  be 
made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  Instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos.  Lithographs.  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  52  Indexes  In  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter.  It  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  Indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  "Copyright  by  Joseph 
Adams.  Vlncennes,  Ind.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  57465,  August  26,  1907 ;  2  copies  received 
August  19,  1907."

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  The  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  2  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day 
of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title.  If  printed  In  full,  would  read :  **  Copyright  by 
American  lithographic  company,  New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  H,  XXc,  no.  9S772,  Aug.  %. 
1907;  2. copies  received  Aug.  26,  1907."

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 


Under  this  heading  is  given  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
Intended  to  be  perfected  as  worlcs  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copy- 
right by  Gregory  Seymour  Allen.  Suffern,  N.  Y.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  23108,  September  28, 
1007 ;  1  photograph  received  September  28,  1007.

ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS,  AND  PRINTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPHS;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS,  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Entries  appears  each  week.  As  the  Issue  of  Part  4  woald 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets  for  the  different  subject-mattem 
contained  In  this  Part — namely,  BngraTlngs,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromes,  Lithographs.  Pho* 
tographs,  and  worlds  of  the  Fine  Arts— It  nas  been  suggested  that  to  avoid  the  incon- 
venlence  of  searching  such  a  number  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  Include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  Instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromes,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  62  Indexes  In  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter.  It  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  Indexes  during  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full  would  read,  ''Copyright  by  W.  L. 
Allen,  Butler,  Pa.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  68542,  October  1,  1907;  2  copies  received  Sep- 
tember  25,    1907.

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 


Under  this  beading  is  given  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  according  to  law  with 
applications  for  copyright  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  deslgni 
Intended  to  be  perfected  as  works  of  the  line  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  suEkalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreriated;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  Is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  drst  title.  If  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copy- 
right by  Vlncenso  Alfano,  New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  23379,  October  12,  1907; 
1  photograph  received  October  12,  1907.

ENGRAVINGS.  CUTS,  AND  PRINTS;  CHROMOS  AND 
LITHOGRAPHS;  PHOTOGRAPHS;  FINE  ARTS 


CLASS  F.  ENGRAVINGS.  CUTS.  AND  PRINTS 

The  Catalogue  of  Copyright  Bntrles  appears. each  week*.  As  the  Issue  of  Part  4  would 
necessitate  the  printing  of  a  number  or  small  alpliabets  for  the  different  subject-matterti 
contained  in  this  Part — ^namely/-  Bngravlngs,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs.  Pho- 
tographs, and  works  of  the  Fine  Arts — It  has  been  suggested  that  to  a^old  the  incon- 
▼enlence  of  searching  such  a  number'  of  small  alphabets  this  Part  of  the  Catalogue  be 
made  to  include  all  the  titles  entered  during  each  month  instead  of  each  week  for 
Engravings,  Cuts,  Prints,  Chromos,  Lithographs,  Photographs,  and  works  of  the  Fine 
Arts.  Instead,  therefore,  of  a  possible  examination  of  52  indexes  in  the  case  of  each 
subject-matter,  it  will  now  be  necessary  to  examine  only  12  indexes  daring  the  year. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  proprietors  with  subalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated ;  the  statement  as  to 
receipt  of  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  day  of  entry. 
The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full  would  read,  "Copyright  by  J.  Allred, 
Jr.,  Toccoa,  Ga.  Class  F,  XXc,  no.  60144,  December  4,  1907 ;  2  copies  received  Decem- 
ber 4,  1907.

CLASS  I.  FINE  ARTS 

Under  this  heading  Is  glTen  a  list  of  the  photographs  deposited  accordins  to  iaw  witii 
applications  for  copyriscbt  of  paintings,  drawings,  statuary,  and  models  or  designs 
intended  to  be  perfected  as  works  of  the  fine  arts.  Arranged  alphabetically  by  pro- 
prietors with  snoalphabet  of  titles. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated;  the  statement  as  to 
the  receipt  of  one  copy  is  always  omitted  when  the  one  copy  has  been  received  on  the 
day  of  entry.  The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  In  full,  would  read :  "  Copy- 
right by  Henry  H.  Ahl.  Boston,  Mass.  Class  I,  XXc,  no.  23815,  November  29,  1907; 
1  photograph  received  November  29,  1907.
